message.content:
print the linking cmd when V=1 
make bench-sin V=1

content.content:
1→# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
     2→# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
     3→#
     4→# Usage:
     5→#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
     6→#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
     7→#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
     8→#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
     9→#   make bench-all      — build, run opt vs ref with EN_PERF, show cycle counts
    10→#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function
    11→#
    12→# Toolchain selection:
    13→#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
    14→#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain
    15→
    16→XLEN ?= 32
    17→
    18→ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
    19→  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
    20→  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
    21→  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
    22→  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
    23→  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
    24→else
    25→  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
    26→  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
    27→  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
    28→  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
    29→  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
    30→endif
    31→
    32→CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    33→AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    34→LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    35→
    36→CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
    37→ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
    38→LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
    39→LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes
    40→
    41→SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
    42→NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
    43→AR   = $(PREFIX)ar
    44→
    45→# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
    46→ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
    47→ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)
    48→
    49→# Assembly sources — single-precision
    50→ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
    51→           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
    52→           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
    53→           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
    54→           sf_fpclassify.s
    55→ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
    56→
    57→# Assembly sources — double-precision
    58→S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
    59→             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
    60→             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
    61→             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
    62→             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
    63→             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
    64→S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
    65→
    66→# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
    67→# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.
    68→
    69→# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
    70→FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
    71→               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
    72→               modff fpclassifyf
    73→
    74→# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
    75→FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
    76→                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
    77→                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo
    78→
    79→# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
    80→FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
    81→                     hypot remainder lrint lround
    82→
    83→# All functions to test
    84→FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)
    85→
    86→# Test output directory
    87→TESTDIR = test
    88→
    89→# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
    90→V ?= 0
    91→ifeq ($(V),1)
    92→  Q =
    93→  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
    94→  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
    95→  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
    96→  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
    97→else
    98→  Q = @
    99→  MSG_AS =
   100→  MSG_CC =
   101→  MSG_LD =
   102→  MSG_AR =
   103→endif
   104→
   105→libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
   106→	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^
   107→
   108→# --- Pattern rules ---
   109→
   110→%.o: %.s
   111→	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
   112→
   113→$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
   114→	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
   115→
   116→$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
   117→	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<
   118→
   119→# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
   120→# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
   121→# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
   122→define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
   123→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
   124→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)
   125→
   126→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   127→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   128→
   129→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   130→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   131→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
   132→	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   133→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   134→	else \
   135→		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
   136→			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
   137→	fi
   138→
   139→size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
   140→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
   141→endef
   142→
   143→# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
   144→define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
   145→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
   146→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)
   147→
   148→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   149→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   150→
   151→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   152→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   153→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
   154→	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   155→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   156→	else \
   157→		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
   158→			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
   159→	fi
   160→
   161→size-$(1): s_$(1).o
   162→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
   163→endef
   164→
   165→# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
   166→define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
   167→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   168→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   169→
   170→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   171→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   172→	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
   173→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   174→	else \
   175→		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
   176→	fi
   177→endef
   178→
   179→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
   180→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
   181→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))
   182→
   183→# Bench rules: compile with -DEN_PERF, run perf() path, display cycle counts
   184→define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE
   185→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
   186→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)
   187→
   188→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
   189→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   190→
   191→bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
   192→	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
   193→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf < /dev/null
   194→	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
   195→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf < /dev/null
   196→endef
   197→
   198→define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT
   199→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o s_$(1).o libandes.a
   200→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)
   201→
   202→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
   203→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   204→
   205→bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
   206→	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
   207→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf < /dev/null
   208→	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
   209→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf < /dev/null
   210→endef
   211→
   212→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE,$(f))))
   213→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
   214→
   215→# --- Top-level targets ---
   216→
   217→OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
   218→           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
   219→REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)
   220→
   221→all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)
   222→
   223→test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
   224→	@echo "Done."
   225→
   226→bench-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
   227→           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))
   228→	@echo "Done."
   229→
   230→size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
   231→	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
   232→	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
   233→		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
   234→			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
   235→	@echo "  --------------------"
   236→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
   237→		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')
   238→
   239→list:
   240→	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
   241→	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"
   242→
   243→clean:
   244→	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_bench.txt
   245→
   246→.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
   247→        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
   248→        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f)) \
   249→        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
   250→        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))
   251→

file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all      — build, run opt vs ref with EN_PERF, show cycle counts
#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# Bench rules: compile with -DEN_PERF, run perf() path, display cycle counts
define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf < /dev/null
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf < /dev/null
endef

define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf < /dev/null
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf < /dev/null
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

bench-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_bench.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))

content.text:
The issue: when `V=1`, `MSG_LD` starts with `@`, which suppresses the entire recipe line (including the LD command after the `;`). Setting `Q=` doesn't help because the `@` from `MSG_LD` takes precedence.

Fix: clear `MSG_LD` when `V=1` so `Q=` causes make to echo the raw LD command.

content.content: The file /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile has been updated successfully.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all      — build, run opt vs ref with EN_PERF, show cycle counts
#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# Bench rules: compile with -DEN_PERF, run perf() path, display cycle counts
define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf < /dev/null
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf < /dev/null
endef

define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf < /dev/null
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf < /dev/null
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

bench-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_bench.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))

content.text: With `MSG_LD =` and `Q =`, the recipe becomes just `$(LD) -o $@ ...` with no `@` prefix, so make echoes the full link command.

lastPrompt: print the linking cmd when V=1  make bench-sin V=1
